steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. Read through: the passage first describes two tribal cultural centers (Tulalip and Citizen Potawatomi), then contrasts them with non-Indigenous-led museums. A correct answer must capture both parts in sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "It discusses two cultural centers operated by tribes, then compares them with non-Indigenous institutions that present Indigenous exhibits.": This matches exactly: paragraphs 1–2 cover the two tribal centers; the final sentence introduces the contrast with non-Indigenous museums. ✓
  • Choice B — "It describes how tribal cultural centers designed exhibits of a particular set of art, then analyzes how non-Indigenous institutions designed exhibits of the same artifacts.": The passage does not mention a particular set of art, and the non-Indigenous museums are not shown to display the same artifacts as the tribal centers. ✗
  • Choice C — "It outlines an early strategy for exhibit design used by one tribal cultural center, then exploits a newer strategy used by a different tribal cultural center.": The passage does not frame the two centers as using different or successive strategies, and both are described using similar approaches. ✗
  • Choice D — "It examines how tribal citizens responded to exhibits at tribal cultural centers, then speculates how non-Indigenous audiences would respond to the same exhibits.": The passage does not describe audience responses at all; it focuses on design philosophy and audience assumptions, not reactions. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
